docs.title: Sakthi's Learning Journal
docs.text: A living knowledge base built from 5+ years of real-world BillingCenter development — production incidents, architectural decisions, and hard-won Gosu patterns.

docs.title: Hi, I'm Sakthi 👋
docs.text:
I'm a Senior Software Engineer specializing in Guidewire BillingCenter — one of the most financially complex platforms in the insurance industry. I work at the intersection of insurance domain knowledge and technical implementation, helping carriers configure, extend, and integrate the Guidewire suite.

My day-to-day spans bug fixes and root cause analysis, configuration and Gosu development, production support, architecture design, and cross-suite integrations. This journal is where I document what I learn — so it's useful to me, and hopefully to others working in the same space.

docs.title: What's Here
docs.text: Section What You'll Find Architecture xCenter architecture, integration flows, design decisions Concepts BillingCenter domain knowledge — invoicing, payment plans, delinquency Gosu Patterns Reusable code patterns, pitfalls, and best practices RCA Library Real bug analyses — root cause, fix, and regression notes AI & Privacy Local LLM setups and privacy-focused AI tooling

docs.title: Core Focus Areas
docs.text:
• BillingCenter — Invoicing, Payment Plans, Delinquency, Commissions, Disbursements
  • Gosu Development — Plugin implementations, GORM queries, bundle handling
  • Cross-Suite Integration — PC ↔ BC ↔ CC flows, REST/Messaging patterns
  • Production Support — RCA, incident response, regression analysis
  • Privacy-Focused AI — Local LLM tooling for secure development workflows

docs.title: How I Use This Journal
docs.text:
• RCAs are written at the time of every significant bug fix or production incident
  • Concepts are added when I learn something not obvious from documentation
  • Patterns are extracted from repeated solutions across tickets
  • Architecture entries are written after design discussions or post-mortems

Want to connect? Find me on GitHub or see my About page.

docs.title: Sakthi S
docs.text:
Senior Software Engineer — Guidewire BillingCenter Specialist

📍 Bengaluru, India  |  🔗 GitHub  |  ✉️ sakthivel.selvakumara@gmail.com

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
Senior Software Engineer with 5+ years of experience across Guidewire BillingCenter development and production support. Started my career in infrastructure and Linux administration, then transitioned into insurance technology — specializing in BillingCenter configuration, Gosu development, and production incident management.

Currently supporting a live carrier environment at ValueMomentum, managing billing jobs, payment processing, and integrations for IDFB Insurance. Previously at Capgemini, delivered end-to-end BillingCenter configurations across multiple client projects and contributed to a major version upgrade from Elysian to Mammoth.

I believe in OOTB-first thinking, upgrade-safe code, and documenting everything worth knowing — which is why this journal exists.

docs.text:
Guidewire Platform

  • BillingCenter (BC) — Expert
  • PolicyCenter (PC) — Working knowledge
  • ClaimCenter (CC) — Working knowledge
  • Gosu scripting — Expert
  • PCF / UI Configuration — Proficient
  • GORM / Query framework — Proficient
  • Plugin development — Proficient

BillingCenter Domains

  • Invoicing & Invoice Streams
  • Payment Plans & Installment Billing
  • Delinquency Workflows & Cancellation
  • Commission & Producer Management
  • Disbursements & Refunds
  • Cross-suite Integration (PC ↔ BC ↔ CC)
  • Version upgrades (Elysian → Palisades)

Production Support

  • Billing job monitoring & incident triage
  • Root cause analysis using logs and SQL
  • SLA-driven priority incident handling
  • Production deployment support & release validation
  • Cross-team coordination (Infra, DB, QA, Business)

Infrastructure & Automation

  • Linux server administration (RHEL / Ubuntu)
  • Shell scripting & task automation
  • System monitoring, patching, user management
  • Technical documentation & SOPs

docs.title: Senior Software Engineer — Guidewire BillingCenter (Production Support)
docs.text:
ValueMomentum  |  Bengaluru, India  |  Nov 2025 – Present

Providing production support for IDFB Insurance's BillingCenter system — owning the stability and reliability of live billing operations.

  • Monitor billing jobs, payment processing, and system integrations
  • Analyze and resolve production issues using application logs and SQL
  • Perform root cause analysis and fix configuration or data issues
  • Handle priority incidents within defined SLA windows
  • Support production deployments and validate post-release stability
  • Coordinate with Infra, DB, QA, and Business teams for end-to-end issue resolution

docs.title: Guidewire Configuration Developer → Senior Software Engineer — BillingCenter
docs.text:
Capgemini  |  Chennai, India  |  Dec 2022 – Oct 2025

Joined as a Software Engineer and grew to Senior Software Engineer over three years of BillingCenter delivery work.

  • Developed and configured billing functionalities in Guidewire BillingCenter
  • Implemented enhancements using Gosu scripting across multiple client projects
  • Fixed defects related to data and configuration migrations
  • Contributed to a major platform version upgrade — Elysian to Mammoth
  • Participated in Agile ceremonies, sprint planning, and peer code reviews
  • Supported pre-production issue resolution and UAT cycles

Started part-time alongside college, transitioned to full-time as bandwidth allowed. A hands-on role spanning both infrastructure and training.

  • Managed Linux servers — installation, patching, and user administration

How to add a new certification

Step 1 — Get your badge image

Source What to do Local file Drop the image (.png, .svg, .webp) into docs/assets/badges/ Credly Open your badge → Share → copy the Image URL and Public URL Guidewire / Badgr Open your badge → right-click the image → Copy image address

Step 2 — Copy the right template from the comments above

  • Local file → use <img src="assets/badges/YOUR_FILE.png" …/>
  • Provider URL → use <img src="https://…/image.png" …/> wrapped in an <a> tag

Step 3 — Fill in the card fields, uncomment the block, save and push.

docs.title: Guidewire Suite Architecture
docs.text: End-to-end architecture of the Guidewire Insurance Suite — xCenter internals, inter-system flows (PC → BC → CC), Contact Manager, and external integration patterns.

docs.title: 1. The Guidewire Insurance Suite — Big Picture
docs.text:
┌─────────────────────────────┐
                         │     Guidewire Platform       │
                         │  (Shared Infrastructure)     │
                         │  Auth │ Messaging │ Search   │
                         └──────────────┬──────────────┘
                                        │
          ┌─────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────┐
          │                             │                             │
┌─────────▼──────────┐      ┌───────────▼──────────┐     ┌──────────▼──────────┐
│   PolicyCenter     │      │   BillingCenter       │     │   ClaimCenter       │
│   (PC)             │      │   (BC)                │     │   (CC)              │
│                    │      │                        │     │                     │
│  Quotes │ Bind     │      │  Invoicing │ Payments  │     │  FNOL │ Reserves   │
│  Endorse│ Renew    │◄────►│  Delinquency│Commissions◄───►│  Payments│Recovery │
│  Cancel │ Audit    │      │  Disbursement          │     │  Subrogation        │
└─────────┬──────────┘      └───────────┬────────────┘     └──────────┬──────────┘
          │                             │                              │
          └─────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────┘
                                        │
                         ┌──────────────▼──────────────┐
                         │      Contact Manager         │
                         │  (Shared Contact Repository) │
                         │  Persons │ Companies │ Roles │
                         └──────────────┬──────────────┘
                                        │
                         ┌──────────────▼──────────────┐
                         │      External Systems        │
                         │  Payment Gateways │ GL       │
                         │  ISO/OFAC │ Vendors │ Portals │
                         └─────────────────────────────┘

One-line summary per xCenter: - PC — What is insured, for how much, under what terms - BC — Collect the premium for it - CC — Pay the loss when something goes wrong, recover what you can - Contact Manager — Who everyone is (shared across all xCenters)

docs.title: 2. Contact Manager — The Shared Foundation
docs.text: Contact Manager (CM) is the central contact repository across the entire suite. Every person or organization referenced in PC, BC, or CC is ultimately a Contact in CM.

docs.title: What CM Stores
docs.text: Entity Examples Person Named insured, claimant, adjuster, producer Company Insurance carrier, vendor, repair shop, law firm Roles PolicyHolder, Producer, Vendor, Claimant, LienHolder

docs.title: CM Integration Points
docs.text:
PolicyCenter                 Contact Manager               ClaimCenter
    │                               │                           │
    │── Named Insured ─────────────►│◄── Claimant ─────────────│
    │── Producer / Agent ──────────►│◄── Vendor / Attorney ────│
    │── Additional Insured ────────►│◄── Adjuster ─────────────│
    │                               │                           │
BillingCenter                       │
    │── Account Holder ────────────►│
    │── Billing Contact ───────────►│

docs.title: Key CM Principles
docs.text:
• Single source of truth — update a contact once, reflected across all xCenters
  • Role-based — same Person can be a PolicyHolder in PC and a Claimant in CC
  • Address/Phone/Email — centrally managed, not duplicated per xCenter
  • Duplicate detection — CM merges duplicate contacts to avoid data sprawl
// Business Purpose: Look up or create a contact in Contact Manager before assigning to policy
// Edge Case: Same person may already exist with slightly different name spelling
var contact = gw.api.database.Query.make(Person)
    .compare("TaxID", Relop.Equals, taxId)
    .select().FirstResult

if (contact == null) {
  contact = new Person(bundle)
  contact.FirstName = firstName
  contact.LastName  = lastName
  contact.TaxID     = taxId
}

docs.title: Core Responsibility
docs.text: Policy lifecycle management — submission to issuance, renewal, endorsement, cancellation, and audit.

docs.title: PC Key Domains
docs.text: Domain Purpose Product Model Defines LOBs, coverages, conditions, exclusions Submission Engine Quote → Bind → Issue lifecycle Policy Transactions Endorsements, cancellations, rewrites, audits Underwriting Rules Eligibility, referral, approval gates Rating Engine Premium calculation per coverage Job Framework All transactions modeled as Jobs (auditable) Account Insured entity, holds policies Producer / Agency Distribution channel, commission tracking

docs.title: PC Policy Lifecycle Flow
docs.text:
Account Created
      │
      ▼
Submission (Job)
      │
   Quote ──► UW Review ──► Referral / Approve
      │
      ▼
   Bind ──► Issue
      │
      ├──► Renewal Job         (system-initiated at expiry)
      │
      ├──► Endorsement Job     (mid-term change)
      │
      ├──► Cancellation Job ──────────────────────────────┐
      │         │                                         │
      │    (Non-Pay) ◄── BC triggers via Delinquency      │
      │                                                   │
      ├──► Rewrite Job         (replace cancelled)        │
      │                                                   │
      └──► Audit Job           (verify exposure post-expiry)
                │                                         │
                ▼                                         ▼
         Audit Charge/Credit ──────────────────► BillingCenter

docs.title: Core Responsibility
docs.text: Premium collection — invoicing, payments, delinquency, commissions, and disbursements.

docs.title: BC Domain Model (Data Hierarchy)
docs.text:
Account
 └── PolicyPeriod (linked from PC)
      └── BillingInstruction
           └── Charge
                └── InvoiceItem
                     └── Invoice
                          └── Payment / Disbursement

docs.title: BC Core Subsystems
docs.text: Subsystem Purpose Invoice Engine Generates invoices from billing instructions Payment Engine Applies payments, handles unapplied cash Delinquency Engine Triggers and manages delinquency workflows Commission Engine Calculates and tracks producer commissions Disbursement Engine Handles refunds and outbound payments

docs.title: BC Configuration Points
docs.text:
configuration/
├── config/
│   ├── BillingRules.xml           ← billing rule sets
│   ├── PaymentAllocationRules     ← how payments are applied
│   └── DelinquencyRules           ← triggers, plans, actions
├── gsrc/
│   ├── gw/plugin/                 ← plugin implementations
│   └── gw/bc/                     ← custom Gosu logic
└── resources/
    └── typelists/                 ← custom typelist extensions

docs.title: Core Responsibility
docs.text: End-to-end claim lifecycle — FNOL through closure, reserves, payments, and recoveries.

docs.title: CC Key Domains
docs.text: Domain Purpose FNOL Intake, policy lookup, coverage match Claim Master claim record, loss date, cause Exposure Coverage-specific unit of claim handling Reserve Financial estimate per exposure (loss + expense) Transaction Payments, recoveries, reserve changes Check Payment instrument generated from transactions Subrogation Recovery from responsible 3rd parties Vendor External service providers (repair shops, doctors) Activity Tasks assigned to adjusters, vendors, specialists

docs.title: CC Claim Lifecycle Flow
docs.text:
FNOL Received
      │
      ▼
Policy Lookup ──► Coverage Verification ──► Exposure Created
      │
      ▼
Claim Assigned (Adjuster / Team)
      │
      ▼
Reserves Set (Loss + Expense per Exposure)
      │
      ├──► Investigation
      │          │
      │    Liability Determined
      │          ▼
      ├──► Evaluation ──► Settlement Negotiation
      │
      ▼
Payment Issued ──► Check Generated ──► Vendor / Claimant
      │
      ├──► Subrogation / Salvage Recovery ──► BC (Recovery Credit)
      │
      ▼
Claim Closed ──► (Reopen if needed)

docs.title: PC → BC: Policy Lifecycle Events
docs.text:
PC Event               BC Action
────────               ─────────
Policy Issued    ──►  Create Account + PolicyPeriod
                       Apply Payment Plan
                       Generate Invoice Stream

Endorsement      ──►  Charge / Return Premium
                       Adjust Scheduled Items

Renewal          ──►  New Term Charges + New Invoice Stream

Cancellation     ──►  Flat / Pro-rata Return Premium
                       Stop Future Invoices

Audit Finalized  ──►  Audit Charge or Credit
                       Settle Invoice Stream

Reinstatement    ──►  Restore charges + past due amount

Plugin hook — BC receives BillingInstruction from PC:

// Business Purpose: Custom validation when billing instruction arrives from PC
// Edge Case: Mid-term endorsements create amendment charges, not new instructions
class BillingInstructionPlugin implements IBillingInstructionPlugin {

  override function beforeCreateBillingInstruction(
      instruction : BillingInstruction) : void {

    var account = instruction.PolicyPeriod.Account
    if (account.TotalOutstandingDue > THRESHOLD) {
      throw new BillingException("Account has unresolved balance — cannot bind")
    }
  }
}

docs.title: BC → PC: Delinquency-Triggered Cancellation
docs.text:
BillingCenter                          PolicyCenter
    │                                       │
    │── Delinquency workflow escalates ───► │
    │── Cancellation request (REST) ──────► │── Cancels PolicyPeriod
    │◄─ Confirmation ─────────────────────  │
    │── Updates delinquency status          │

docs.title: PC → CC: Policy Data for Claims
docs.text:
PC Data Available in CC at FNOL
────────────────────────────────
- Policy coverages at time of loss
- Coverage limits / deductibles
- Named insureds / additional insureds
- Producers / agents
- Policy period (effective / expiration)
- LOB-specific exposure data

docs.title: CC → BC: Recovery Credits
docs.text:
CC Event               BC Action
────────               ─────────
Recovery Received ──►  Credit transaction on policy account
Salvage Received  ──►  Credit applied to claim cost offset

docs.title: Deductible Collection (CC → BC)
docs.text:
ClaimCenter                           BillingCenter
    │                                      │
    │── Deductible due (REST) ───────────► │── Creates deductible charge
    │                                      │── Adds to next invoice
    │◄─ Payment confirmed ─────────────── │

docs.title: Integration Architecture Overview
docs.text:
┌──────────────┐    REST (sync)     ┌──────────────────┐
│   External   │ ◄────────────────► │   xCenter        │
│   System     │    Messaging       │   (PC / BC / CC) │
└──────────────┘ ◄────────────────► └──────────────────┘
                    Plugin/Event
Pattern When to Use Example Synchronous REST Real-time, response needed immediately Payment gateway auth Async Messaging Fire-and-forget, high volume Policy bound → BC Plugin Intercept internal behavior Custom payment allocation Batch / File Legacy systems, bulk data Premium bordereaux export

docs.title: REST Integration (Inbound to BC)
docs.text:
External System ──► Integration Gateway ──► BillingCenter API

POST /bc/rest/apis/v1/accounts/{accountId}/payments
Authorization: Bearer <token>
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "amount": 1500.00,
  "currency": "USD",
  "paymentMethod": "CreditCard",
  "referenceNumber": "TXN-20240101-001",
  "effectiveDate": "2024-01-15"
}

// Business Purpose: Validate inbound payment from payment gateway before recording
// Edge Case: Duplicate reference numbers from gateway retries
class ExternalPaymentPlugin implements IPaymentPlugin {

  override function validatePayment(payment : Payment) : List<String> {
    var errors = new ArrayList<String>()
    var existing = findByReferenceNumber(payment.ReferenceNumber)
    if (existing != null) {
      errors.add("Duplicate payment reference: ${payment.ReferenceNumber}")
    }
    return errors
  }
}

docs.title: Async Messaging (Outbound from BC)
docs.text:
BillingCenter ──► Message Queue (JMS) ──► GL System
                                     └──► Notification Service
                                     └──► Data Warehouse
Event Trigger Consumer InvoiceDue Invoice becomes due Notification service PaymentReceived Payment applied ERP / GL system DelinquencyInitiated Delinquency starts CRM PolicyCancelled BC-initiated cancellation Data warehouse

docs.title: Plugin-Based Integration (Key BC Plugins)
8. xCenter Comparison
docs.text: Dimension PolicyCenter BillingCenter ClaimCenter Core Object Policy / Job Account / Invoice Claim / Exposure Transaction Model Policy Jobs Financial Transactions Reserve / Payment Transactions Workflow Driver UW Rules + Approval Payment Plans + Delinquency Adjuster Assignment + SLA Key Integration Out BC (charges), CC (policy data) GL, Payment Gateway BC (recovery), Vendors Key Integration In Agent Portals, Rating PC (policy events) PC (coverage), Vendors Financial Impact Premium determination Premium collection Loss payment

docs.title: 9. Cloud vs On-Premise
docs.text:
Aspect On-Premise Guidewire Cloud Deployment Customer-managed servers Guidewire-managed AWS Integration Direct DB / SOAP / JMS REST via Integration Gateway Configuration Full filesystem access Studio + App Events Upgrades Manual, infrequent Continuous delivery Extension points Plugins, Gosu, PCF App Events, Accelerators, REST

Cloud Constraint

In Guidewire Cloud, outbound calls cannot be made directly from Gosu plugins. All external calls must go through Integration Gateway or App Events.

docs.title: 10. Core Architecture Principles
docs.text:
• Never bypass GORM — no raw SQL. All data access through entities.
  • Plugins over core changes — extend via plugin interfaces only.
  • Bundle discipline — always control transaction scope. Avoid nested bundles.
  • Upgrade safety — gsrc/ and config/ survive upgrades; core edits do not.
  • OOTB first — evaluate platform capability before writing custom logic.
  • Contact Manager — never duplicate contact data across xCenters; always reference CM.

docs.title: RCA Library
docs.text: Root cause analyses from real production and UAT incidents.

docs.title: How to Use
docs.text:
1. Copy template.md
  2. Rename: YYYY-MM-DD-short-description.md
  3. Place in rca-library/incidents/
  4. Add entry to the table below

docs.title: Incident Log
docs.text:
Date Component Severity Summary YYYY-MM-DD Module P1/P2/P3 Short description

Tip

Writing RCAs within 24 hours of resolution gives the best recall. Even a 10-minute write-up is valuable.

docs.title: RCA Template
docs.text: Copy this file for every new incident. File naming: YYYY-MM-DD-short-description.md

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: One sentence: what broke, in which module, with what business impact.

docs.title: Issue
docs.text: Component: <!-- e.g. InvoiceDueDate calculation, PaymentPlan charge generation --> Environment: <!-- DEV / QA / UAT / PROD --> Detected: <!-- Date --> Severity: <!-- P1 / P2 / P3 -->

docs.title: Expected Behavior
docs.text: What should have happened, from both a business and technical perspective.

docs.title: Actual Behavior
docs.text:
What actually happened. Include any error messages, stack traces, or incorrect data.

<!-- Paste stack trace or error log here -->

docs.title: Root Cause
docs.text:
The precise technical reason — not symptoms, the actual cause.

Key Insight

Highlight the single most important finding here.

docs.title: Business Impact
docs.text:
• Policies affected:
  • Financial impact:
  • User impact:
  • SLA breach: Yes / No

docs.title: Approach
docs.text: Why this fix was chosen over alternatives.

docs.title: Code Changes
docs.text:
// Reason For Change: 
// Edge Case:

// BEFORE
// ...

// AFTER
// ...

docs.title: Files Changed
docs.text: File Change Type Notes

docs.title: Risks & Regression
docs.text: Risk Likelihood Mitigation

docs.title: Test Scenarios
docs.text:
• [ ] Happy path: ...
  • [ ] Edge case: ...
  • [ ] Regression: ...

docs.title: Timeline
docs.text: Time Event Issue detected Investigation started Root cause identified Fix deployed Verified in production

docs.title: Lessons Learned
docs.text:
What would have prevented this? What will you do differently?
